You wrote this essay in 2019
titled The Bitter Lesson and
this is the most influential
essay perhaps in the history
of AI. But people have used
that as a justification for
scaling up LLMs because in
their view this is the one
scalable way we have found to
pour ungodly amounts of
compute into learning about
the world. And so it's
interesting that your
perspective is that the LLMs
are actually not bitter or
less pill. >> It's an
interesting question whether
large language models are are
a case of the bitter lesson.
>> Yeah. >> Because they are
clearly a way of using massive
computation things that will

up to the limits of the
internet. >> Yeah. but they're
also a way of putting in a
lots of human knowledge and so
so this is an interesting
question it's a sociological
or industry question will they
reach the limits of of of the
data and and be superseded by
things that that are can get
more data just from experience
rather than from from people.
, in some ways it's a classic
case of the of the of the
bitter lesson with the more
the more human knowledge we
put into the large language
models, the better they can
do. And so it feels good. And
yet one well I in particular
expect there to be systems

altogether why we need a whole
